verdict.answer_first: Tie: both lead with the outcome, A: "It's an infinite loop." B: "It's an infinite loop."
verdict.decisiveness: B commits more clearly: "The integer-counter version is better"; A surveys "Fixes, roughly in order of preference" with 3 options.
verdict.fabrication: B invents/gets wrong the end state: "until x overflows to Infinity"; in practice addition stops changing first. A's "around 2^53" is approximate but directionally grounded.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to apply because it names the preferred fix after code: "integer-counter version is better"; A is more complete but more option-heavy.
